text.title: Trend Lifecycle and STEEP Framework
text.summary: Trend types, lifecycle stages, optimal entry timing, and the STEEP scoring rubric for evaluating trend viability.

rule.statement: Classify every trend as Fad (weeks-months), Trend (2-5 years), Megatrend (decades), or Shift (permanent) before scoring it — solopreneur sweet spot is the Trend category.
rule.rationale: Fads do not sustain a business. Megatrends and Shifts are too broad to carve a defensible niche quickly. 2-5 year trends offer the largest opportunity window relative to required investment.

list.item: Fad: weeks to months (fidget spinners, NFT art)
Trend: 2-5 years (remote work tooling, no-code platforms)

rule.statement: Enter just before mainstream adoption — after the Early Adopter stage confirms demand but before incumbents have locked distribution.
rule.rationale: Innovator stage: too early, no market. Late mainstream: commoditization and shrinking margins. The Early-to-Mainstream inflection is where CAC is still low and pricing power exists.

list.item: Innovator: define category — risk: too early, no market
Early adopter: high margins — risk: must educate the market
Early mainstream: growth — risk: competition increasing (optimal entry point)

rule.statement: Apply the STEEP framework (Social, Technology, Economic, Environmental, Political) to every trend before scoring; accept "n/a" for irrelevant dimensions rather than padding.
rule.rationale: Agents fill all five STEEP cells even when Environmental is irrelevant — forcing irrelevant cells produces noise that inflates the score. Explicit "n/a" is honest and faster to audit.

list.item: Social: How are behaviors and values changing? Which demographics are shifting?
Technology: What new tech enables this trend? What capabilities became cheap enough?
Economic: What financial forces drive it? What cost arbitrages exist?
Environmental: How does sustainability affect adoption? Are there regulatory tailwinds?
Political: What regulations impact it? Which governments are accelerating or blocking?

rule.statement: Compute the weighted Trend Strength Score (growth rate 25%, adoption stage 20%, incumbent count 15%, investment activity 20%, personal fit 20%) before the counter-signal critic runs; treat any score above 4.0 as requiring an adversarial review pass.
rule.rationale: Bullish-only pipelines recommend "pursue" on every trend. A high score without a counter-signal critic is a confirmation bias loop.

example.note: AI Agents trend scored in 2024: Google Trends +500% in 6 months (growth 5/5), Early mainstream stage (stage 4/5), 50+ incumbents (incumbents 2/5), $2B+ funding (investment 5/5), personal fit varies by team (fit 3/5). Weighted total: 4.1/5 — triggers adversarial review.
Accepting a 5/5 score on all criteria without a single cited source URL — the rubric becomes a self-reinforcing fantasy, not a decision tool.
